verdict_reason: The engineering layer correctly identifies brāhmī sthiti and brahma-nirvāṇa as metaphysically maximally loaded technical terms and does not attempt to engineering-translate them. The three doctrinal readings (Advaita: nondual mokṣa; Vishishtadvaita: devotion-qualified abiding plus entry; Gaudiya: Vaikuṇṭha) are preserved at the source-pack level and the engineering layer explicitly stays at the operational rung — the senior's career-state stability — which is tradition-neutral and does not flatten any tradition into another. The STRETCHED tag and MEDIUM confidence honestly mark the metaphysical scope-mismatch. The falsifiability clause is precisely the kind of disclaimer an Indian-philosophy-aware reading produces ('the metaphysical scope of brāhmī sthiti and brahma-nirvāṇa exceeds anything an operational analog can describe; an engineer who reads this verse as become unflappable through experience has read it half-correctly at the operational layer and entirely incorrectly at the metaphysical layer'). This is the model treatment for a metaphysically loaded verse; no conflation, no flattening. Pass.
